methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
1X Technologies, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Bernt Øivind Børnich, is currently valued at $10.0B post-money after raising $123.5M to date. The valuation framework draws on a peer set of 5 same-stage private robotics companies—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), Fairmat ($350M), and GrayMatter Robotics ($300M)—alongside 1 public comparable, covering a range of $300M to $1.8B among peers.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression modeling, anchored to the $10.0B last post-money and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a forward valuation of $19.0B. Peer-median valuation, derived from the 5 same-sector comparables, yields a median of $500M with a range of $300M to $1.8B, substantially below the current mark. The divergence between methods reflects 1X's outlier position relative to the peer cohort on recent valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $425M (low) to $21.9B (high), with a median of $9.8B. The current $10.0B valuation aligns closely with the median estimate. Key uncertainties center on the applicability of stage-progression assumptions to an outlier-valued company and the representativeness of the peer set, which skews smaller and may not fully capture 1X's capital intensity or go-to-market trajectory.
